# Keeper registry persistence: created instances survive a reload — serialize/rehydrate through the accept path

## Context

T4.1 of the harness pillar (#13349) — the persistence line every registry decision reserved for: `blueprintSnapshot` is registry-owned + structured-clone-safe, records are history-complete (disposed kept), and the accept path is the ONE re-entry door. A page reload currently loses every created widget; the wedge's product promise ("a stranger types an intent and gets a live app") needs the app to still be there.

## The Fix

- Serialize: the registry's live+disposed records (the snapshot IS the serialization source — the field was designed for this) to a durable store. v1 target: the session-scoped persistence the framework's app-worker offers; the durable-git/T4-full story stays out (this is reload-survival, not history).
- Rehydrate on create-module boot: for each persisted LIVE record, replay `acceptBlueprint(record.blueprintSnapshot, record.instanceId)` through the UNCHANGED accept path — same validator, same stage seam, same insert-registrar (idempotent: the duplicate-id pre-check refuses if a record already re-registered). Disposed records restore as registry history WITHOUT instantiation.
- The provider's flow state rehydrates from the outcome (any live instances → `materialized`), keeping the SSOT's boot-truth rule: render state derives from what EXISTS, never from a persisted flow flag (a persisted snapshot-of-flow-state would be the exact snapshot-as-self class the identity work just banned — state re-derives from the trail of records).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Round-trip unit: register 2 + dispose 1 → serialize → clear → rehydrate → 1 live instance re-accepted through the real path, 1 disposed record restored as history, resolution semantics identical pre/post.
- [ ] Rehydration validates EVERY snapshot through `validateBlueprint` again (a tampered/stale persisted payload refuses bounded — persistence is an input, inputs are never trusted).
- [ ] Flow state derives from rehydration outcome, never persisted directly.
- [ ] Idempotent double-rehydrate (the duplicate-id refusal proves it).

## Out of Scope

Durable/git-backed persistence + cross-session history (the full T4 line, its own leaf when the product needs it) · persisting external (non-keeper) instances · migration of persisted payloads across schema versions (registry versioning carries it: `grid@1` refuses under a future `grid@2`-only registry — correct and loud).

## Related

Parent #13349 (T4.1) · the registry (merged — snapshot ownership + history-complete were built FOR this) · the accept path (merged — the ONE re-entry door) · the SSOT's boot rule · the snapshot-as-self ban (recorded, identity epic).

Claimable — non-Fable implementable; every contract it consumes is merged and unit-pinned.

[ARCH_ALIGNMENT]

Intake verdict: `needs-design` / `not-code-ready` for implementation pickup.

V-B-A performed before classification:
- Read the live #14766 conversation: created 2026-07-04T13:14:08Z, still open, unassigned, no comments; stale labels absent and `no auto close` absent, so this is pre-stale metadata only.
- Read the parent #13349 epic comments: @neo-gpt already posted an epic-review on 2026-06-15, and @neo-fable's 2026-07-04 horizon map places reload/rehydrate in T4. The parent prerequisite is satisfied, but it does not define #14766's storage contract.
- Queried the ticket KB for #14766: it restates the intended serialize/rehydrate-through-accept-path shape and says consumed contracts are merged.
- Queried Memory Core for this exact #14766 framing: no prior raw-memory mapping found.
- Read the core-idiom anchors required for instance/reactive-state work: `src/core/Base.mjs`, `src/Neo.mjs`, and `src/state/Provider.mjs`.
- Read the live implementation anchors: `CreatedInstances.mjs`, `acceptPath.mjs`, `CreationStateProvider.mjs`, and `creationFlowState.mjs`.
- Searched source/tests for persistence surfaces. The existing anchors are registry/accept/provider only. The only obvious browser persistence surface is `Neo.main.addon.LocalStorage` and AgentOS theme usage; I did not find an existing session-scoped app-worker persistence contract for created-instance registry state.
- Read `learn/agentos/process/contract-ledger.md`: public/consumed surfaces require a T3 Contract Ledger before branch/code work.

Why this is not code-ready as written:
1. The ticket introduces a consumed persistence contract: storage backend/key, serialized envelope, live/disposed record shape, rehydrate ordering, stale/tampered payload refusal, duplicate-id/idempotence behavior, and provider flow derivation. The issue body has no Contract Ledger matrix for those surfaces.
2. The phrase "session-scoped persistence the framework's app-worker offers" is not anchored to a current implementation surface. The source I found exposes `Neo.main.addon.LocalStorage` remotely to the app worker, which is localStorage-shaped, not an already-defined session-scoped registry persistence contract.
3. The existing registry and accept path are good anchors, but the new boot-time rehydrate seam is not yet specified: where it runs, what storage namespace it reads, how disposed history is restored without instantiation, and how invalid persisted rows are retained/refused are all review-time arguments unless ledgered first.

Route back to code-ready:
- Add a Contract Ledger covering at least: persistence backend + storage key, serialized registry envelope/version, live record rehydrate through `acceptBlueprint`, disposed-history restore without instantiation, invalid/tampered payload handling, duplicate-id/idempotence behavior, and provider `flowState` derivation from rehydrate outcome.
- Surface-anchor each row: `CreatedInstances`, `acceptBlueprint`, `validateBlueprint`, `CreationStateProvider.applyCreationRouteOutcome` / `applyFlowEvent`, and the exact storage API chosen (`Neo.main.addon.LocalStorage` or a new/explicit session-scoped substrate).
- Decide whether v1 is reload-survival via localStorage, tab-session survival via sessionStorage, or a new app-worker store; encode that in the ticket before implementation.

After that, #14766 can be claimed as a narrow implementation leaf instead of forcing storage semantics to be invented during PR review.
